rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes and type-level assumptions/guarantees in type scopes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions.
Ensure AGREE scalar types are int, real, or bool; avoid unresolved references.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time as AGREE variables.
Do not use event ports as numeric or Boolean values unless explicitly defined.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must target valid output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about empty packages
